perturbation.original_text: 1.7 "Products" shall mean the products offered by JRVS for sale to the Distributor; the Products shall include iMine cryptocurrency mining rigs and its parts and components.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.7 "Products" shall mean only fully assembled cryptocurrency mining rigs offered by JRVS for sale to the Distributor. Individual parts and components are excluded from this definition unless explicitly stated otherwise in a subsequent written agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change narrows the definition of 'Products' to exclude individual parts and components unless specifically included in a separate agreement. This introduces ambiguity because the original definition included 'its parts and components,' and other sections may rely on the broader definition. This contradicts with section 7.2, where it states that if a Distributor determines any unit of Products defective, the Distributor shall promptly notify JRVS of such defects. The change, causes confusion in cases where a component is found to be defective, as the component is no longer considered a 'product'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.2 Inspection and Acceptance. The Distributor shall have thirty (30) days (the "Inspection Period") upon receipt of each shipment to inspect and test the Products. If the Distributor determines any unit of Products defective, the Distributor shall promptly notify JRVS of such defects. Defective Products may be returned for retest, evaluation and examination subject to JRVS Returned Material Authorization ("RMA") procedure; provided, that such written notification and request for an RMA number must be received by JRVS during the Inspection Period. Returns must be prepaid by Distributor. When requesting a return authorization, Distributor must supply Distributor's purchase order number and JRVS's invoice number. Product description must include lot number and wafer numbers.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Products' to exclude individual parts and components unless specifically included in a separate agreement, while the original definition included 'its parts and components'. Section 7.2 allows the Distributor to return defective Products. Because the components are no longer considered products under the new definition in 1.7, it introduces a strong contradiction, impacting the ability of the distributor to return defective components.

perturbation.original_text: 3.8 Inventory. The Distributor shall maintain an inventory of Products in reasonably sufficient quantities to provide adequate and timely delivery to the Customers. At a minimum, such inventory shall include not less than the quantity of Products necessary to meet Distributor's reasonably anticipated demands for a thirty (30) day period.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.8 Inventory. The Distributor shall maintain an inventory of Products, specifically only complete mining rigs, in quantities deemed appropriate by JRVS to ensure adequate and timely delivery. The term 'adequate' is determined solely at JRVS's discretion, considering market conditions and JRVS's production capabilities, without necessarily aligning with Distributor's sales forecasts.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by giving JRVS sole discretion over what is considered 'adequate' inventory, overriding the original intent which ties it to the Distributor's anticipated demands. This is an in-text contradiction with section 4.1, where the Distributor must provide a good faith rolling quarterly sales forecast for the units of the Products. The change suggests that despite the sales forecast, the Distributor must agree with the quantity of Products being deemed appropriate by JRVS.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 Rolling Forecast. The Distributor shall provide JRVS at least one month in advance with a good faith rolling quarterly sales forecast for the units of the Products to be provided by JRVS to the Distributor hereunder during each month in such calendar quarter. Subject to Section 4.4, not later than the 15t h day of each month, Distributor will provide JRVS with a binding purchase order for the third month following the month in which the purchase order is placed and a non-finding forecast for the following three months (i.e., the fourth, fifth and sixth months following the month in which the forecast is given. 2 Source: IMINE CORP, S-1, 7/25/2018
perturbation.justification: The changed text grants JRVS sole discretion over 'adequate' inventory, overriding the Distributor's sales forecasts outlined in section 4.1. This directly contradicts the requirement in 4.1 for the Distributor to provide a good faith rolling quarterly sales forecast, creating an inconsistency between the Distributor's obligation to forecast sales and JRVS's power to determine inventory independently of that forecast.

perturbation.original_text: 12.2 Termination for Convenience. This Agreement may be terminated by either party for any reason or no reason, whether or not extended beyond the initial term, by giving the other party written notice ninety (90) days in advance.
perturbation.changed_text: 12.2 Termination for Convenience. JRVS may terminate this Agreement for any reason or no reason, whether or not extended beyond the initial term, by giving the Distributor written notice ninety (90) days in advance. However, the Distributor may only terminate this agreement if JRVS breaches a material term of the agreement and fails to cure the breach within 30 days of written notice.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces a clear contradiction by creating an asymmetrical termination right. Previously, both parties could terminate for any reason with 90 days' notice. Now, the Distributor's termination is limited to JRVS's breach of contract, creating an unfair power balance. This contradicts with section 12.6, where it discusses the no liability to either party, which such limitations can be debatable based on the power balance that it has. This is because one party, can be free to not fulfill their contractual responsibilities, whereas the other is forced to abide by it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.6 No Liability. In the event of termination by either party in accordance with any of the provisions of this Agreement, neither party shall be liable to the other, because of such termination, for compensation, reimbursement or damages on account of the loss of prospective profits or anticipated sales or on account of expenditures, inventory, investments, leases or commitments in connection with the business or goodwill of either party. Termination shall not, however, relieve either party of any obligations incurred prior to the termination, including, without limitation, the obligation of the Distributor to pay JRVS for Products purchased prior to such termination.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an asymmetrical termination clause. The original text allowed either party to terminate for any reason with 90 days' notice. The changed text limits the Distributor's termination rights to situations where JRVS breaches the contract, creating an imbalance. This contradicts section 12.6, which states that neither party shall be liable to the other in the event of termination. The new termination clause creates a situation where JRVS could potentially act in bad faith, knowing that the Distributor's only recourse is to prove a material breach, which may be difficult and costly. This undermines the "no liability" provision because the Distributor's exposure is now significantly greater than JRVS's, who is free to terminate at will.
